architecture.summary: A taxonomic and design-pattern framework for hybrid neuro-symbolic systems. The paper does not propose a single system but rather analyzes and categorizes existing hybrid architectures through modular design patterns. It identifies elementary patterns (basic building blocks for combining statistical and symbolic methods) and compositional patterns (patterns that combine elementary patterns) that can be composed to describe the architecture of diverse hybrid systems. Information flow varies by pattern: some integrate symbolic constraints into neural forward/backward passes; others route between separate statistical and symbolic engines via data or intermediate representations.

biblio.title: Modular design patterns for hybrid learning and reasoning systems

claims.statement: A large body of recent literature on hybrid neuro-symbolic AI systems is highly diverse, mostly empirical, and lacks a unifying view
A very large number of hybrid systems can be described by composing only a small set of elementary patterns as building blocks
15+ design patterns for hybrid AI systems can be organized into a set of elementary patterns and a set of compositional patterns
The design patterns reveal similarities between systems that were not recognized until now
The patterns extend and refine Kautz's earlier attempt at categorizing neuro-symbolic architectures
